pi_items.source: Verified via live environment testing: Preferences > Tech setup shows GF Low = 30% as Subsurface default for Buhlmann algorithm (screenshot 09_preferences_tech_setup_gf.png). Also confirmed as industry-standard conservative setting (Suunto default is 30/70 per suunto.com docs). The task asks the agent to SET this value, which matches the default — effectively a no-change confirmation for GF Low.

The task requires the agent to change GF High from the Subsurface default of 75% to 70%. Live testing confirmed default GF High = 75% in Preferences > Tech setup (screenshot 09_preferences_tech_setup_gf.png). The target value of 70 is a standard conservative setting used in technical diving (GF 30/70 is the Suunto default per suunto.com). The verifier checks for gfhigh in range [68,72] in Subsurface.conf, which correctly validates GF High = 70.

Live testing confirmed the Dive Planner's Bottom SAC field shows 20 L/min as the default value (screenshot 04_dive_planner_open.png, visual grounding confirmed at coordinates ~715,482). Web sources (scubascribbles.com, DAN) indicate typical SAC rates range 14-27 L/min; 20 L/min is a standard planning value for active bottom work. This value is what the agent must ensure is set.

The task requires setting Deco SAC to 15 L/min. Live testing of plan_tech_deco_dive trajectories shows the Subsurface default Deco SAC is 17 L/min (agents had to change it to 15). Web sources indicate deco SAC rates are typically 11-17 L/min; 15 is a standard conservative planning value. The agent must change the default from 17 to 15.

30m is a standard recreational depth limit and appropriate for a baseline dive in a tech training progression. PADI Open Water max depth is 18m, Advanced is 30m, making 30m the natural 'recreational baseline' as labeled. The verifier checks depth range [25,35]m which centers on 30m.

30 minutes at 30m on EAN32 is within no-decompression limits (NDL for EAN32 at 30m is approximately 30-40 minutes depending on GF settings), making it appropriate for a recreational baseline dive. Consistent with task description.

EAN32 (Enriched Air Nitrox with 32% O2) is the most common nitrox blend in recreational diving, confirmed by multiple sources (Wikipedia 'Nitrox', ScubaBoard, aqua-marina.com). MOD at PO2 1.4 is ~33.75m, making it safe for a 30m dive. Standard notation is EAN32 or Nitrox 32.
EAN32 is a binary nitrogen-oxygen mix with 0% helium by definition. No helium is used in recreational nitrox blends.

12-liter cylinders are the standard size in European technical diving. Subsurface defaults to 12L in the Dive Planner (confirmed during live testing of gas table). This is a standard aluminum 80 or steel 12L cylinder.

200 bar is the standard working pressure for European diving cylinders. Subsurface defaults to approximately 200 bar (203 bar shown in live testing, screenshot 04_dive_planner_open.png). The slight difference (200 vs 203) is within normal fill variation.

40m is the standard depth for PADI Tec 40 course (store.padi.com) and is the natural next step in a progressive training series after 30m. At this depth, decompression obligations begin on EAN32 for bottom times over ~15 minutes, making it appropriate for 'Introduction to Decompression'.

25 minutes at 40m on EAN32 will incur decompression obligations, appropriate for an intro-to-deco training dive. NDL for EAN32 at 40m is approximately 10-15 minutes, so 25 minutes ensures mandatory deco stops.

EAN32 at 40m gives PO2 = 0.32 * 5.0 = 1.60 bar, which is at the maximum PO2 limit. This is the upper edge but commonly used in tech training at this depth. Some agencies prefer Air at this depth, but EAN32 is standard for PADI TecRec.

EAN50 (50% O2) is the standard decompression gas for dives in the 30-50m range. Web sources (aqua-marina.com, scubaboard.com) confirm EAN50 as a standard deco gas. MOD for EAN50 at PO2 1.6 is 22m, suitable for deco stops from 21m upward.
50m matches the PADI Tec 50 course depth limit (confirmed at store.padi.com/tec-50 and divingacademy.com). It is the standard 'gateway' depth for trimix training, requiring helium to manage narcosis.

20 minutes at 50m on Trimix 21/35 produces a total runtime of approximately 55-65 minutes including decompression (confirmed by Kimi-K2.5 agent trajectory on plan_tech_deco_dive which produced 61-minute total runtime with these same parameters). This is a standard training dive duration.

Trimix 21/35 uses 21% O2, giving PO2 = 0.21 * 6.0 = 1.26 bar at 50m, safely under the 1.4 limit. Confirmed as a standard bottom gas mix by multiple sources (tdisdi.com, scubaboard.com: '190'-21/35' in standard gas tables).

Trimix 21/35 contains 35% helium, reducing the nitrogen fraction to 44%. At 50m, the Equivalent Narcotic Depth (END) = (50+10) * (1-0.35) - 10 = 29m, keeping narcosis below the commonly accepted 30m END limit. Multiple web sources confirm 21/35 as a standard mix for 50m dives.
EAN50 as intermediate deco gas for deep dives, used from 21m upward. Standard technical diving practice confirmed by web sources.

Pure oxygen (100% O2) as shallow deco gas, used at 6m and 3m stops. MOD at PO2 1.6 is 6m. Standard technical diving practice. The PADI Tec Trimix course explicitly covers 'up to 4 stage/deco cylinders' including oxygen (store.padi.com).

Arbitrary string defined by the task creator. Consistent with task description. The verifier checks for 'trimix' or 'dahab' or 'training' (case-insensitive) in the trip location attribute, which this name satisfies.

The Blue Hole in Dahab, Egypt is a real and famous dive site on the Red Sea coast of the Sinai Peninsula (en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blue_Hole_(Red_Sea)). It is one of the world's most well-known dive sites and a realistic location for technical dive training.

Multiple web sources place the Blue Hole at latitude 28.5700-28.5728°N. latlong.net gives 28.5728°N; latitude.to gives 28.5700°N. The metadata value of 28.5720°N falls squarely within this range. Verified as accurate.

Web sources place the Blue Hole at longitude 34.5358-34.5369°E (latlong.net: 34.5369; latitude.to: 34.5358). The metadata value of 34.5412°E is approximately 500m east of these references, possibly placing the pin at the shore entry point rather than the center of the hole. The verifier uses generous tolerance (±0.5°), so this does not affect scoring. For maximum accuracy, 34.537 is closer to authoritative sources, but 34.5412 is within the Dahab coastline area.

Arbitrary name defined by the task creator. 'Ahmed Hassan' is a common Egyptian name, appropriate for a Dahab-based dive instructor scenario. The verifier checks for 'ahmed' or 'hassan' case-insensitively in the buddy field.

Verified during live testing that /home/ga/Documents/ exists, is owned by user ga, and is writable (test file created and deleted successfully). Subsurface's Print dialog supports 'Print to file (PDF)' with configurable output path (screenshot 11_print_to_pdf_dialog.png). The verifier checks for file existence and size > 1024 bytes at this path.

Live environment testing confirmed SampleDivesV2.ssrf contains exactly 29 dives across 4 trips (Hoodsport Dec 2010: 4 dives, Hoodsport Sep 2011: 6 dives, Larnaca Oct 2011: 6 dives, Koror Jun 2013: 11 dives, plus 2 standalone dives). Verified by XML parsing: sum(1 for _ in root.iter('dive')) = 29. The setup_task.sh records this as baseline in /tmp/plan_tech_training_series_initial_dive_count. After the agent plans 3 dives, expected count is 32.

Live testing confirmed Subsurface's default GF High is 75% (not 70%). The agent must actively change this value from 75 to 70 in Preferences > Tech setup. This is an important data quirk: the task says 'set GF High 70%' but the default is 75%, so the agent must recognize this needs changing.

Agent trajectory analysis of plan_tech_deco_dive (Gemini run_1 and Kimi run_0) confirmed the default Deco SAC in the Dive Planner is 17 L/min. The agent must change this to 15 L/min. Steps 4-11 of the Gemini trajectory show repeated attempts to change the Deco SAC from 17 to 15.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses the official Subsurface SampleDivesV2.ssrf dataset (from github.com/subsurface/subsurface/tree/master/dives) containing exactly 29 dives across 4 trips. The agent must plan 3 new dives using the Dive Planner (expected final count: 32 dives). Key ground truth: (1) GF settings in Preferences > Tech setup — default GF Low is already 30% (no change needed), but default GF High is 75% and must be changed to 70%. (2) SAC rates — default Bottom SAC is 20 L/min (no change needed), but default Deco SAC is 17 L/min and must be changed to 15 L/min. (3) Dive 1: 30m depth, 30 min, EAN32 (32% O2, 0% He) — single cylinder. (4) Dive 2: 40m depth, 25 min, two gases — EAN32 bottom gas + EAN50 (50% O2) deco gas. (5) Dive 3: 50m depth, 20 min, three gases — Trimix 21/35 (21% O2, 35% He) bottom gas + EAN50 deco + pure Oxygen (100% O2) deco. All cylinders 12L/200bar. (6) Trip name: 'Advanced Trimix Course - Dahab 2024'. (7) Location: Blue Hole, Dahab, Egypt with GPS approximately 28.572°N, 34.537°E (verifier tolerance ±0.5°). (8) Buddy: 'Ahmed Hassan' on all 3 planned dives. (9) PDF export to ~/Documents/tech_training_plan.pdf (must be > 1024 bytes). (10) The Dive Planner marks planned dives with divecomputer model containing 'plan' in the SSRF XML. A successful 50m/20min dive on Trimix 21/35 with EAN50+O2 deco produces approximately 55-65 minutes total runtime including decompression stops.
